appraisal_events.evidence: « il aimait la sincérité, mais il l’aimait comme une proxénète… » ; « En somme il mentait autant qu’Odette… il n’était pas moins égoïste. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator explicitly undercuts Swann by exposing his hypocrisy: he condemns lying yet lies to extract truths from Odette, framing his ‘love of sincerity’ as self-serving.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, Swann’s moral standing drops as the narrator indicts his hypocrisy and self-interested pursuit of ‘truth’.
